title: L&A Clientwise — Competitors Serving L&A Insurance Clients
grain: one row per competitor engaged at a given L&A insurance client (client-to-competitor pairing)
summary: Lists, for each L&A insurance client, the competing service providers active at that client, an approximate size of each competitor's footprint (mostly revenue ranges), the segment/function the competitor supports, and free-text research notes on what the competitor does there. It lets you see which competitors are present at a named client and roughly how large their presence is.
use_cases: Which competitors are engaged at a given L&A client (e.g. Prudential)?
At which L&A clients does a specific competitor (e.g. Accenture) appear?
What is the approximate revenue or FTE footprint of a competitor at a client?
Which functions or segments does a competitor support at an L&A client?
How many distinct competitors are listed against each L&A client?

columns.name: Client Name
columns.note: L&A insurance client; populated only on the first row of each client's block, so 78% null — forward-fill needed to attribute the competitor rows below it to the correct client

columns.name: Competitor
columns.note: competing service provider at the client

columns.name: FTEs/Revenue
columns.note: approximate size of the competitor's footprint; mostly revenue ranges stored as text (e.g. '$20-$30M', '$40M'), not a clean numeric measure

columns.name: Segment supported by competitor
columns.note: short tags for the function/segment the competitor supports (e.g. 'Tech, Consulting', 'Digital Ops')

columns.name: CoPilot Research
columns.note: multi-line research notes on the competitor's role across insurance value-chain functions; mostly empty

not_answerable.q: What is the L&A client's own premium or revenue?
not_answerable.reason: no column carries the client's financials; FTEs/Revenue describes the competitor's footprint, not the client

not_answerable.q: How has a competitor's footprint at a client changed over time?
not_answerable.reason: there is no date or time-series column; the table is a single snapshot

not_answerable.q: What is EXL's own scope or revenue at these clients?
not_answerable.reason: no column captures the host provider's engagement, only competitors' presence

warnings: vintage_unknown — no date found in filename, sheet names, headers, or cell values; data owner should confirm the as-of date
near-duplicate column set with 'P&C Clientwise' — same schema but distinct data (L&A clients vs P&C clients); not a duplicate
Client Name is sparse (78% null): the client name appears only on the first row of each client's competitor block, so forward-fill is required to associate competitor rows with their client
